Well, AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon are forming a joint venture to work towards eliminating dead zones in the U.S. by pooling spectrum and leaning on satellite connectivity, particularly in rural and underserved areas. The carriers say that they will share limited spectrum to boost capacity, improve satellite integration for rural operators, and create a unified platform which would make it easier for satellite providers to reach customers with consistent coverage.
